name: eslint-plugin-prettier
usage_summary: Not directly required in config.js, but loaded transitively via @shopify/eslint-plugin's configs.prettier spread (shopifyPlugin.configs.prettier in config.js line 260). That upstream config requires eslint-plugin-prettier and enables the 'prettier/prettier' rule as an error. The explicit declaration in eslint-plugin-cli's package.json is redundant but makes the dependency resolvable even if @shopify/eslint-plugin's peer resolution changes.
how_to_remove: Drop the explicit 'eslint-plugin-prettier' entry from eslint-plugin-cli/package.json. The package will still be available transiently through @shopify/eslint-plugin (which requires it in its own prettier config). If @shopify/eslint-plugin ever stops bundling it, the 'prettier/prettier' rule would silently stop applying. Alternatively, switch to relying solely on eslint-config-prettier (already also used) which disables conflicting rules without running prettier as a lint rule — a pattern recommended by the prettier project itself.
replacement_reason: The explicit declaration is redundant since @shopify/eslint-plugin already pulls in eslint-plugin-prettier. Removing the direct dep reduces surface area without any functional change. If the team wants to drop prettier-as-a-lint-rule entirely, eslint-config-prettier (already present) is sufficient for disabling conflicting rules.
